prompt_tree.title: Rigorous Reconstruction of the Grand Synthesis Thesis
prompt_tree.description: A task tree of prompts to transform the sketch into a fully rigorous mathematical work, addressing every missing proof identified in the referee report.

root.prompt: Begin the rigorous reconstruction of the thesis. Follow the child tasks in order, generating complete LaTeX with definitions, lemmas, theorems, and fully detailed proofs.

local_mtc.title: Part I: Local MTC-Weighted Transfer Operator
local_mtc.description: Rigorously construct the local Hilbert space, operators, and prove the determinant identity.

weighted_hilbert.prompt: Define the local Hilbert space H_p = l^2(E⃗) ⊗ (⊕_{k≥2} V_k) with a rapidly decaying weight λ_k = 2^{-k}. Specify the inner product precisely. Prove that the direct sum is a separable Hilbert space. Define the algebraic core of finite vectors.

ribbon_projector.prompt: Define the ribbon operator W as a diagonal multiplication operator using θ_{k,j} and d_{k,j}. Prove it is bounded. Define the Verlinde vacuum projector Π_0 using S-matrix entries and D_k. Prove it converges in operator norm on the weighted space, is a self-adjoint projection, and commutes with W.

supertrace_identity.prompt: Prove the key lemma: Str_MTC(Π_0 W^n) = 1 for all n ≥ 1. This must be an operator-theoretic proof using the explicit Hilbert-Schmidt or trace-class properties of the weighted completion. Include a careful handling of the graded trace and show that cancellations from S-matrix unitarity yield exactly 1. [Detailed steps: set up the trace, evaluate trace of Π_0 W^n, use Verlinde formula, show all non-vacuum terms cancel, address convergence of the sum over k,j.]

regularized_sdet.prompt: Define the regularized superdeterminant sdet(1 - L_{p,s}) for the weighted operator. Choose a regularization (e.g., zeta-regularization or Fredholm determinant). Prove that it factorizes as det_*(1 - L_{p,s}) * F_p(s), where the geometric part gives 1-p^{-s}. Using the supertrace identity, prove F_p(s)=1. Conclude the local Euler factor theorem.

adelic_global.title: Part I: Adelic Gluing and Global Determinant
adelic_global.description: Construct the adelic Hilbert space and prove the global superdeterminant identity.

restricted_tensor_product.prompt: Construct the restricted tensor product H = ⊗'_p H_p with respect to the vacuum vectors Ω_p (the unique vectors in the image of Π_0 with tree vacuum). Use the Guichardet/von Neumann framework. Define the global algebraic core A_0. Show that local operators extend to H and that the global superdeterminant factorizes as a product of local ones (in the sense of regularized products).

archimedean_place.prompt: Develop the archimedean factor rigorously. Define a 'continuous MTC' for SL(2,R): take a direct integral of principal series representations with Plancherel measure, define a vacuum vector (K-invariant), a ribbon operator W_∞, and a projector Π_0∞. Prove that the regularized superdeterminant of (1 - L_{∞,s}) times the classical Gamma factor equals 1. Show that this matches the completed zeta function's archimedean part.

global_sdet.prompt: Combine all places: prove that the adelic superdeterminant equals ∏_{p<∞} (1-p^{-s}) · (π^{s/2}/Γ(s/2)) = \hat{ζ}(s)^{-1}. Provide a complete proof using the factorization property and the archimedean result.

functional_equation.prompt: Prove that the restricted tensor product is self-dual under the adelic Fourier transform. Deduce the functional equation \hat{ζ}(s) = \hat{ζ}(1-s) directly from the operator construction, without appealing to the known analytic continuation of the zeta function.

spectral_triple.title: Part II: Construction of the Supersymmetric Spectral Triple
spectral_triple.description: Define the global Dirac operator and prove the spectral triple axioms.

dirac_definition.prompt: Define the local operator D_p = i (d/ds) log L_{p,s} |_{s=1/2} rigorously. Show that the family s ↦ L_{p,s} is a holomorphic family of bounded operators in a strip around s=1/2. Use the functional calculus to define the logarithm and its derivative in the strong sense. Provide an explicit expression for D_p in terms of the tree adjacency and the MTC ribbon operator.

selfadjointness.prompt: Prove that D_p is essentially self-adjoint on the dense core of finite-depth vectors with finite MTC level. Use the Kato-Rellich theorem with an explicit number operator N_p (tree depth plus MTC level operator). Derive uniform estimates independent of the cut-off using the vacuum projector. Conclude that the closure \overline{D}_p is self-adjoint.

global_D.prompt: Assemble the global operator D = ⊕'_p D_p on the restricted tensor product core. Show that it is essentially self-adjoint on the algebraic core A_0 using Nelson's analytic vector theorem. Provide the construction of a global number operator N and the required commutator estimates. Include the archimedean D_∞ and its self-adjointness.

bounded_commutators.prompt: Define the algebra A_0 of finite Hecke correspondences. For every a ∈ A_0, prove that [D, a] extends to a bounded operator on the completed Hilbert space. Provide explicit commutator estimates using the local structure.

compact_resolvent.prompt: Prove that (1 + |D|)^{-1} is a compact operator on H. Show that the resolvent factorizes and each local factor is compact; for the archimedean part, use the known compactness of the resolvent of the Casimir operator.

supersymmetry.prompt: Prove the grading relation Γ D = -D Γ, where Γ is the global Z_2-grading inherited from MTC parities. Show that Γ is a bounded self-adjoint involution and that it anticommutes with D.

superdeterminant_identity.title: Part II: Zeta-Regularized Superdeterminant Identity
superdeterminant_identity.description: Link the spectral triple's superdeterminant to the completed zeta function.

zeta_regularized_sdet.prompt: Define the zeta-regularized superdeterminant det_ζ(1 - s D^{-1}). Set up the zeta function ζ_D(z) = Str_Γ(|D|^{-z}) for Re(z) large, prove it admits a meromorphic continuation, and define the regularized determinant. Provide the heat kernel representation and show the asymptotic expansion needed for the Hadamard product.

factorization.prompt: Prove that ζ_D(z) factorizes as a product of local zeta functions over all places (including the archimedean). Conclude that det_ζ(1 - s D^{-1}) = ∏_p' sdet(1 - L_{p,s}) · R_∞(s).

link_to_zeta.prompt: Using the earlier global superdeterminant identity, prove the key equality: det_ζ(1 - s D^{-1}) = \hat{ζ}(s)^{-1} as entire functions.

spectrum_rh.title: Part II: Spectrum Identification and the Riemann Hypothesis
spectrum_rh.description: Prove that the spectrum of D is exactly the non-trivial zeros on the critical line.

hadamard_factorization.prompt: For an operator with compact resolvent, prove that det_ζ(1 - s D^{-1}) has a canonical Weierstrass product expansion ∏_ρ (1 - s/ρ) e^{s/ρ} (with possible exponential factors coming from regularization). Derive this from the heat kernel asymptotics.

zero_set_equality.prompt: From det_ζ(1 - s D^{-1}) = \hat{ζ}(s)^{-1} and the Hadamard factorization of both sides, prove that the multi-set of eigenvalues of D (counting multiplicity) equals the set of non-trivial zeros of ζ(s). [Carefully handle the ambiguity of exponential factors by comparing the divisor and growth.]

positivity.prompt: Prove that all eigenvalues of D lie on the line Re(s)=1/2. Using supersymmetry Γ D = -D Γ and positivity of the vacuum projector, show that any eigenvalue with Re(ρ)≠1/2 would lead to a contradiction in the graded supertrace identity or to a violation of the functional equation. Alternatively, provide a direct proof that D is unitarily equivalent to a skew-adjoint operator plus 1/2, using a chiral decomposition. This step completes the proof of the Riemann Hypothesis within the framework.

obstacle_resolutions.title: Detailed Resolutions of the Four Obstacles
obstacle_resolutions.description: Provide the full pages of analysis that were only sketched in the original Chapter 14.

obstacle1_full.prompt: Write the complete proof for Obstacle 1 (domain and self-adjointness). Include: definition of the number operator N_p, explicit commutator estimates [D_p, N_p], proof that vacuum vectors are analytic, uniform bounds from Π_0, application of Nelson's theorem on the restricted tensor product core, and treatment of the archimedean component.

obstacle2_full.prompt: Write the complete proof for Obstacle 2 (global supertrace convergence). Include: proof that local heat kernels are trace-class after Π_0, Mellin transform justification with absolute convergence of the Euler product in the right half-plane, construction of the global supertrace as a tempered distribution, and resolution of the circularity worry by first proving the determinant identity on the dense subalgebra without using global trace-class properties.

obstacle3_full.prompt: Write the complete proof for Obstacle 3 (spectral pollution and positivity). Include: analytic continuation of the superdeterminant from the zeta function, rigorous proof of Hadamard factorization for the zeta-regularized superdeterminant, uniqueness of the factorization in the class of functions with given zeros and growth, proof that supersymmetry and the Verlinde positivity force spectrum onto Re(s)=1/2 (e.g., by showing D - 1/2 is skew-adjoint on a suitable subspace).

obstacle4_full.prompt: Write the complete proof for Obstacle 4 (ribbon residuals). Include: proof that Π_0 W = id on the full completed local space (not just the algebraic core), verification that the Guichardet product's gluing map commutes with Π_0, and demonstration that no finite-order corrections appear at low MTC levels.
